raw_response:
{
  "internal_reasoning": "This work is consistent in subject, facture, and compositional language with Njideka Akunyili Crosby’s highly sought-after mature early-2010s practice, a period that many collectors and institutions regard as foundational to her market and art-historical standing. The image shows a full-length female figure rendered with extraordinary delicacy against a dense photo-transfer collage ground, integrating painting, drawing, and layered appropriated imagery. The title, date, and described thematic emphasis on domesticity, Nigerian-American visual hybridity, and postcolonial identity place the work squarely within the artist’s most important and institutionally validated body of work. Akunyili Crosby’s market is driven not only by rarity—she produces slowly and with exacting craft—but also by broad cross-category demand from contemporary collectors, museums, and patrons focused on Black figuration, diasporic narratives, and major women artists of the 21st century.\n\nArtistically, the work appears strong. The central figure is psychologically self-possessed and formally compelling, with nuanced modeling of the face and hands, contrasted with the more decoratively dispersed environment. The pink architectural framing device and patterned lower register create a structurally sophisticated composition, and the collage field behind the figure seems rich in vernacular and mass-media references. That tension between intimate portraiture and saturated cultural memory is one of the key reasons the artist’s best works command premium prices. The scale, while not explicitly given, appears substantial from the full-length figure; larger, resolved compositions generally outperform smaller studies or works on paper. The image suggests a complete, exhibition-ready work rather than a preparatory or secondary example.\n\nCondition cannot be conclusively assessed from the image, but no major tears, abrasions, warping, staining, or restoration are evident. Because Akunyili Crosby’s mixed-media surfaces can be sensitive, actual auction value would depend heavily on a professional condition report confirming stability of the transfers, paper support, surface media, and any mounted elements. Absence of provenance and exhibition history introduces some discount in confidence, though for this exercise authenticity is assumed.\n\nIn market terms, Akunyili Crosby achieved extraordinary auction results beginning in the mid-2010s, with top prices for major figurative interiors reaching into the multi-million-dollar range. Her strongest large-scale paintings from circa 2011–2014 have become particularly desirable because they represent the moment in which her signature language fully crystallized. Comparable high-quality works by the artist have sold at major international auctions broadly from the low millions into the high single-digit millions depending on size, date, exhibition record, and iconicity. The very best and most famous examples can exceed those levels, while less ambitious or smaller works trade materially below them. Based on the visual strength here, the early date, and the title’s resonance, this appears to belong in the upper-middle to upper tier of her auction market.\n\nBalancing those factors, I would place the realistic current fair market auction value at approximately $5.8 million. That estimate assumes the work is authentic, in very good condition, and of substantial scale, but without leveraging any premium from exceptional provenance, museum exhibition, or publication history, each of which could push the result higher. Conversely, if the work proved smaller than it appears, had condition issues in the collage layers, or lacked the visual impact of her best-known domestic interiors in person, the result could fall into the $3.5–$5 million range. With favorable positioning at a major evening sale in New York or London, strong competition could also carry it above my estimate toward $7 million or more.",
  "valuation_usd": 5800000,
  "confidence": 0.78,
  "key_factors": [
    "Strong early-mature 2012 date within Akunyili Crosby’s most coveted formative period",
    "High artistic quality combining portraiture, photo-transfer collage, and cross-cultural narrative complexity",
    "Robust international demand for the artist from collectors and institutions",
    "Likely substantial scale and fully resolved composition rather than a study or minor work",
    "Value sensitive to condition, provenance, and whether the work matches top-tier exhibition-quality examples in person"
  ]
}

parsed.internal_reasoning:
This work is consistent in subject, facture, and compositional language with Njideka Akunyili Crosby’s highly sought-after mature early-2010s practice, a period that many collectors and institutions regard as foundational to her market and art-historical standing. The image shows a full-length female figure rendered with extraordinary delicacy against a dense photo-transfer collage ground, integrating painting, drawing, and layered appropriated imagery. The title, date, and described thematic emphasis on domesticity, Nigerian-American visual hybridity, and postcolonial identity place the work squarely within the artist’s most important and institutionally validated body of work. Akunyili Crosby’s market is driven not only by rarity—she produces slowly and with exacting craft—but also by broad cross-category demand from contemporary collectors, museums, and patrons focused on Black figuration, diasporic narratives, and major women artists of the 21st century.

Artistically, the work appears strong. The central figure is psychologically self-possessed and formally compelling, with nuanced modeling of the face and hands, contrasted with the more decoratively dispersed environment. The pink architectural framing device and patterned lower register create a structurally sophisticated composition, and the collage field behind the figure seems rich in vernacular and mass-media references. That tension between intimate portraiture and saturated cultural memory is one of the key reasons the artist’s best works command premium prices. The scale, while not explicitly given, appears substantial from the full-length figure; larger, resolved compositions generally outperform smaller studies or works on paper. The image suggests a complete, exhibition-ready work rather than a preparatory or secondary example.

Condition cannot be conclusively assessed from the image, but no major tears, abrasions, warping, staining, or restoration are evident. Because Akunyili Crosby’s mixed-media surfaces can be sensitive, actual auction value would depend heavily on a professional condition report confirming stability of the transfers, paper support, surface media, and any mounted elements. Absence of provenance and exhibition history introduces some discount in confidence, though for this exercise authenticity is assumed.

In market terms, Akunyili Crosby achieved extraordinary auction results beginning in the mid-2010s, with top prices for major figurative interiors reaching into the multi-million-dollar range. Her strongest large-scale paintings from circa 2011–2014 have become particularly desirable because they represent the moment in which her signature language fully crystallized. Comparable high-quality works by the artist have sold at major international auctions broadly from the low millions into the high single-digit millions depending on size, date, exhibition record, and iconicity. The very best and most famous examples can exceed those levels, while less ambitious or smaller works trade materially below them. Based on the visual strength here, the early date, and the title’s resonance, this appears to belong in the upper-middle to upper tier of her auction market.

Balancing those factors, I would place the realistic current fair market auction value at approximately $5.8 million. That estimate assumes the work is authentic, in very good condition, and of substantial scale, but without leveraging any premium from exceptional provenance, museum exhibition, or publication history, each of which could push the result higher. Conversely, if the work proved smaller than it appears, had condition issues in the collage layers, or lacked the visual impact of her best-known domestic interiors in person, the result could fall into the $3.5–$5 million range. With favorable positioning at a major evening sale in New York or London, strong competition could also carry it above my estimate toward $7 million or more.
key_factors: Strong early-mature 2012 date within Akunyili Crosby’s most coveted formative period
High artistic quality combining portraiture, photo-transfer collage, and cross-cultural narrative complexity
Robust international demand for the artist from collectors and institutions
Likely substantial scale and fully resolved composition rather than a study or minor work
Value sensitive to condition, provenance, and whether the work matches top-tier exhibition-quality examples in person
